item.title: Pigeons living wall of building 141-147 Dursley Rd, 9th March
item.description:

item.description:

item.title: Car parked live this for a week in the street, 9th March
item.description:
This car has been parked like this for almost a week in the street. It is often parked in a similar manner at different points in the street, always jacked up, and missing a wheel. This is a residential street. How is this safe?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Howard Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light not working since 19.02.26 outside numbers 10 and 11 BR7 6TD, 9th March
item.description:
Reported originally on 20.02.26. Updated last week as still not resolved. Today we saw a Riney vehicle in the road as we were walking with our dog. The driver said he was going to fix the light - we thanked him! One hour later when we returned, the broken light was still untouched and yet Fix My Street states it has been fixed!! We believe the workman looked at another street light that didn’t need attention and went away again! Meanwhile we are left in darkness for who knows how long? PLEASE HELP URGENTLY! Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Windsor Close, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A loose and uneven concrete slab on Farringdon Street outside of Goldman Sachs, 9th March
item.description:
Uneven concrete slab causing a trip hazzard

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stonecutter Street, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Pavement Defect (uneven surface / cracked paving slab)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Park Road, Poverest, Greater London

item.title: Litter not picked for over a year now! this area is consistently missed, 9th March
item.description:
Accumulating unpicked litter in the planted border along Albatross Way, between (and including) the steps linking Dominion Drive to Swan Road and Canada Water station. This area, plus the planted beds that border Seth Street and the Albion Estate, plus Swan Road where it joins Seth Street are consistently neglected. It seems likely that Albatross Way, being a recently constructed waling route, has never been incorporated into a street cleaning route; and its a-joining steps and routes are falling into a grey area between estate and street cleaning schedules. Something must be done about this as the amenity value of the area is deteriorating as a consequence, with rubbish and dumping starting to occur in the streets nearby. The route is the main pedestrian thoroughfare linking Canada Water station and the wooded walkways leading to residential streets towards Surrey Water. It also takes increasingly busy footfall as a cut through for cyclists and walkers between Canada Water and Rotherhithe. Albatross way and the steps have definitely not been litter picked for a year. Albatross Way borders the recent housing development which is maintained privately - but the walkway itself IS the responsibility of Southwark (despite the fact that the Street name sign itself has been demolished) Southwark treescape and street cleaning is generally well attended to but this busy back route is totally neglected. Please litter pick the planted areas before the shrub growing season makes it more difficult and please acknowledge and instate the route as part of the general street cleaning schedule. Residents should not have to repeatedly report this problem - neither should they have to litter pick themselves, even though this often happens. We pay council tax. No-one wants to live in or walk through a dirty, rubbish-strewn neighbourhood and we really shouldn't have to. Its likely an oversight - but it is a long-standing and systemic one and should now be remedied.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swan Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
